mission.title: flux: kustomize v4 high-impact breaking changes
mission.description: kustomize v4 high-impact breaking changes. This issue affects 17+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
The latest release of [kustomize](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/releases) comes with breaking changes that have a high impact on Flux users.

steps.title: Apply the fix for kustomize v4 high-impact breaking changes
steps.description:
We've published an image of kustomize-controller compatible with kustomize v4, please give it try and report all issues to this PR https://github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/pull/343

To test this please use the following
```yaml
$ kustomize version 
{Version:kustomize/v4.0.1 GitCommit:516ff1fa56040adc0173ff6ece66350eb4ed78a9 BuildDate:2021-02-14T05:00:02+00:00 GoOs:darwin GoArch:amd64}

$ kustomize build github.com/fluxcd/flux2/manifests/install?ref=v0.8.0
Error: accumulating resources: accumulation err='accumulating resources from '../bases/source-controller': '/private/var/folders/bh/bp5hplld7g915wdvkt5w0y9w0000gn/T/kustomize-040141028/manifests/bases/source-controller' must resolve to a file': recursed accumulation of path '/private/var/folders/bh/bp5hplld7g915wdvkt5w0y9w0000gn/T/kustomize-040141028/manifests/bases
```

steps.title: Upgrade flux to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade flux:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade flux flux/flux --namespace flux
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux
helm list -n flux
```

steps.title: Confirm kustomize v4 high-impact breaking changes is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
We've published an image of kustomize-controller compatible with kustomize v4, please give it try and report all issues to this PR https://github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/pull/343

To test this please use the following image:

```
ghcr.io/fluxcd/kustomize-controller:kustomize-v4-rc-de0310e7
```

To upgrade kustomize-controller on an cluster bootstrapped with `flux`, add the following
codeSnippets:
$ kustomize version 
{Version:kustomize/v4.0.1 GitCommit:516ff1fa56040adc0173ff6ece66350eb4ed78a9 BuildDate:2021-02-14T05:00:02+00:00 GoOs:darwin GoArch:amd64}

$ kustomize build github.com/fluxcd/flux2/manifests/install?ref=v0.8.0
Error: accumulating resources: accumulation err='accumulating resources from '../bases/source-controller': '/private/var/folders/bh/bp5hplld7g915wdvkt5w0y9w0000gn/T/kustomize-040141028/manifests/bases/source-controller' must resolve to a file': recursed accumulation of path '/private/var/folders/bh/bp5hplld7g915wdvkt5w0y9w0000gn/T/kustomize-040141028/manifests/bases/source-controller': accumulating resources: accumulation err='accumulating resources from 'https://github.com/fluxcd/source-controller/archive/v0.8.0.zip//source-controller-0.8.0/config/crd': yaml: l

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
